soccer.guide.rondo.step1.title: Form the Circle
soccer.guide.rondo.step1.description: Place cones in a circle (about 8-10 meters diameter). Position 5-8 players on the outside and 1-2 defenders in the middle.
soccer.guide.rondo.step2.title: Two-Touch Passing
soccer.guide.rondo.step2.description: Outside players keep the ball away from the defenders using a maximum of two touches. If a defender wins the ball, they swap with the player who lost it.
soccer.guide.rondo.step3.title: One-Touch Challenge
soccer.guide.rondo.step3.description: Progress to one-touch passing only. Players must scan for open teammates before receiving the ball.
soccer.guide.rondo.step4.title: Add Movement
soccer.guide.rondo.step4.description: Outside players can now move along the circle to find better passing angles. Encourage constant adjustment of body position.
soccer.guide.rondo.tip1: Remind players to open their body to see as many teammates as possible before receiving
soccer.guide.rondo.tip2: Encourage defenders to work together and cut passing lanes instead of chasing the ball individually
soccer.guide.triangle-passing.step1.desc: Use cones to mark triangles with sides of about 8 meters. Place one player at each cone. With more players, set up multiple triangles.
soccer.guide.triangle-passing.step2.desc: Pass the ball around the triangle using two touches: one to control, one to pass. Focus on accuracy with the inside of the foot.
soccer.guide.triangle-passing.step3.desc: Progress to direct one-touch passes. Players must be on their toes and have their body already open to the next receiver.
soccer.guide.triangle-passing.step4.desc: After passing, jog to the cone of the player you passed to. This creates continuous rotation and simulates real game movement.
soccer.guide.triangle-passing.tip1: Insist on proper passing technique: plant foot next to the ball, ankle locked, strike through the center
soccer.guide.triangle-passing.tip2: If passes are sloppy, increase the triangle size. Reduce it as technique improves to raise the tempo
soccer.guide.wall-pass.step1.title: Mark the Channel
soccer.guide.wall-pass.step1.description: Set up a 10-meter long and 5-meter wide channel with cones. Place a passive defender (mannequin or cone) in the middle.
soccer.guide.wall-pass.step2.title: Walk Through the Combination
soccer.guide.wall-pass.step2.description: Player A dribbles toward the defender, plays a firm pass to Player B (the wall), then sprints behind the defender. Player B plays a one-touch return pass into the space.
soccer.guide.wall-pass.step3.title: Increase Speed
soccer.guide.wall-pass.step3.description: Repeat at game speed. Emphasize the timing: the pass to the wall must be firm and the sprint must start immediately after releasing the ball.
soccer.guide.wall-pass.step4.title: Add a Live Defender
soccer.guide.wall-pass.step4.description: Replace the passive defender with a real one. The attacker must decide when to play the wall pass and when to dribble past.
soccer.guide.wall-pass.tip1: The pass to the wall player should be played into their front foot so they can lay it off first time
soccer.guide.wall-pass.tip2: The runner must accelerate into the space behind the defender immediately after passing, not wait
soccer.guide.cone-slalom.step1.title: Set Up Cones
soccer.guide.cone-slalom.step1.descripti: Place 8-10 cones in a straight line with about 1.5 meters between each cone. Set up multiple lanes if you have many players.
soccer.guide.cone-slalom.step2.title: Slow Dribble Through
soccer.guide.cone-slalom.step2.descripti: Dribble through the cones at walking pace using the inside and outside of your dominant foot. Focus on keeping the ball close to your feet.
soccer.guide.cone-slalom.step3.title: Alternate Feet
soccer.guide.cone-slalom.step3.descripti: Now use both feet, alternating inside-left then inside-right around each cone. Keep your knees bent and stay low.
soccer.guide.cone-slalom.step4.title: Speed Run
soccer.guide.cone-slalom.step4.descripti: Dribble through as fast as possible while maintaining control. Time each run and challenge players to beat their own record.
soccer.guide.cone-slalom.tip1: Use small touches, not big kicks. The ball should never be more than one step away from your feet
soccer.guide.cone-slalom.tip2: Lean your body in the direction of the turn and use the outside of the foot to push the ball past each cone
soccer.guide.one-v-one.step1.title: Set Up the Channel
soccer.guide.one-v-one.step1.description: Mark a channel about 15 meters long and 5 meters wide with cones. Place a small goal or target at one end. Pair players up by similar ability.
soccer.guide.one-v-one.step2.title: Attacker Starts with the Ball
soccer.guide.one-v-one.step2.description: The attacker starts at one end with the ball. The defender starts 3 meters away facing them. On the whistle, the attacker tries to dribble past and score.
soccer.guide.one-v-one.step3.title: Play Multiple Rounds
soccer.guide.one-v-one.step3.description: Each pair plays 3 rounds, then switch attacker and defender. Encourage attackers to use feints, changes of pace, and body swerves.
soccer.guide.one-v-one.step4.title: Rotate Partners
soccer.guide.one-v-one.step4.description: Rotate so each player faces a different opponent. Discuss which moves worked best and why.
soccer.guide.one-v-one.tip1: Attackers should run at the defender at speed. Slowing down gives the defender time to set their feet
soccer.guide.one-v-one.tip2: Defenders should stay on their toes with a low center of gravity, delaying rather than diving in
soccer.guide.shooting-edge.step1.title: Position the Servers
soccer.guide.shooting-edge.step1.descrip: Place two players with balls near the edge of the penalty area, one left and one right. Position a goalkeeper in the goal. Shooters line up centrally.
soccer.guide.shooting-edge.step2.title: Receive and Shoot
soccer.guide.shooting-edge.step2.descrip: The server plays a firm pass to the shooter who takes a touch to set the ball and shoots with the second touch. Aim for the corners of the goal.
soccer.guide.shooting-edge.step3.title: Alternate Sides
soccer.guide.shooting-edge.step3.descrip: Alternate receiving from the left and right servers. This forces the shooter to adjust body shape and shoot with both feet.
soccer.guide.shooting-edge.step4.title: One-Touch Finishing
soccer.guide.shooting-edge.step4.descrip: Progress to one-touch shooting. The player must already have their body set before the ball arrives.
soccer.guide.shooting-edge.tip1: Keep the body over the ball and the standing foot next to it to keep shots low
soccer.guide.shooting-edge.tip2: Accuracy over power. Pick a corner before you receive the ball
soccer.guide.crossing-finishing.step1.de: Place a cone at each wing position near the byline. Crossers line up wide, finishers line up centrally at the edge of the box.
soccer.guide.crossing-finishing.step2.de: The right winger dribbles to the byline and crosses the ball into the box. Two attackers run into the near post and far post areas to finish.
soccer.guide.crossing-finishing.step3.de: Repeat from the left side. Encourage crossers to vary between low driven crosses and higher lofted balls.
soccer.guide.crossing-finishing.step4.de: Place one or two defenders in the box to challenge the attackers. Finishers must time their runs to arrive as the ball does.
soccer.guide.crossing-finishing.tip1: Crossers should look up before delivering the ball to see where the runners are
soccer.guide.crossing-finishing.tip2: Attackers should start their run from behind the defender and attack the ball at pace
soccer.guide.pressing-trap.step1.title: Explain the Pressing Trigger
soccer.guide.pressing-trap.step1.descrip: Split into two teams of 5-7. Explain that the pressing trigger is a poor touch or a backward pass by the opponent. When it happens, the whole team presses together.
soccer.guide.pressing-trap.step2.title: Walk Through the Shape
soccer.guide.pressing-trap.step2.descrip: Position the pressing team in a compact block. Show how the nearest player applies pressure on the ball while teammates cut off the closest passing options.
soccer.guide.pressing-trap.step3.title: Live Pressing Drill
soccer.guide.pressing-trap.step3.descrip: The possession team tries to play out from the back. The pressing team triggers on the coach's signal and tries to win the ball within 5 seconds.
soccer.guide.pressing-trap.step4.title: Transition to Attack
soccer.guide.pressing-trap.step4.descrip: When the pressing team wins the ball, they must immediately try to score on a mini goal within 3 passes. This trains the quick transition mindset.
soccer.guide.pressing-trap.tip1: The first player to press must sprint to cut off the forward passing lane, not just run at the ball
soccer.guide.pressing-trap.tip2: If the press is broken, the team must quickly drop back and reset its compact shape
soccer.guide.overlap-run.step1.title: Set Up the Wing Zone
soccer.guide.overlap-run.step1.descripti: Mark a 20x15 meter zone along one side of the pitch. Position a winger on the touchline and a full-back about 5 meters behind and inside.
soccer.guide.overlap-run.step2.title: Walk Through the Overlap
soccer.guide.overlap-run.step2.descripti: The winger receives the ball and holds it. The full-back sprints around the outside of the winger. The winger passes into the space ahead of the overlapping runner.
soccer.guide.overlap-run.step3.title: Add Tempo and a Cross
soccer.guide.overlap-run.step3.descripti: Run the same pattern at full speed. The overlapping player delivers a cross into the box where a striker finishes.
soccer.guide.overlap-run.step4.title: Add a Defender
soccer.guide.overlap-run.step4.descripti: Place a defender in the zone. The winger must decide: pass to the overlapping runner or cut inside if the defender follows the overlap.
soccer.guide.overlap-run.tip1: The full-back must time the run to arrive at top speed when the ball is played, not start too early
soccer.guide.overlap-run.tip2: The winger should use a body feint (look inside) to freeze the defender before passing wide
soccer.guide.through-ball.step1.title: Set Up the Passing Lane
soccer.guide.through-ball.step1.descript: Place two lines of cones 2 meters apart to create a defensive line. A passer stands 10 meters behind it. A runner starts level with the line.
soccer.guide.through-ball.step2.title: Timed Runs
soccer.guide.through-ball.step2.descript: The runner starts their forward run and the passer plays the ball through the gap in the cones. The runner must time the run to stay onside and collect the ball.
soccer.guide.through-ball.step3.title: Vary the Weight of Pass
soccer.guide.through-ball.step3.descript: Practice playing through balls with different weight: firm and low for fast runners, lofted for players making deeper runs.
soccer.guide.through-ball.step4.title: Add Passive Defenders
soccer.guide.through-ball.step4.descript: Replace cones with passive defenders who walk the line. The passer must find the gap between defenders and time the pass to the runner's movement.
soccer.guide.through-ball.tip1: The passer must look up and make eye contact with the runner before playing the ball
soccer.guide.through-ball.tip2: The runner should check their shoulder to see the defensive line and time the run to the last moment
soccer.guide.goalkeeper-basics.step1.des: Stand in the middle of the goal with feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, hands at waist height with palms facing forward. This is the set position.
soccer.guide.goalkeeper-basics.step2.des: A partner rolls the ball to the left and right. The keeper gets down by bending the knees (not bending at the waist), scoops the ball with both hands, and brings it into the chest.
soccer.guide.goalkeeper-basics.step3.des: The partner throws the ball at chest and head height. The keeper catches with a W-shape hand position (thumbs almost touching behind the ball) and cushions it into the body.
soccer.guide.goalkeeper-basics.step4.des: Start on your knees. The partner throws the ball to the side. Push off with the nearest leg, extend hands to the ball, and land on your side. Progress to standing dives.
soccer.guide.goalkeeper-basics.tip1: Always get your body behind the ball as a second barrier, even when catching with your hands
soccer.guide.goalkeeper-basics.tip2: On diving saves, the bottom hand goes behind the ball and the top hand goes on top to secure it
soccer.guide.five-v-five.step1.title: Set Up the Pitch
soccer.guide.five-v-five.step1.descripti: Mark a pitch of about 30x20 meters with cones. Place small goals at each end. Split players into two teams of 5 and distribute bibs.
soccer.guide.five-v-five.step2.title: Play with Free Rules
soccer.guide.five-v-five.step2.descripti: Play a normal game. Encourage players to use the width and find the free player. Coach from the side about spacing and movement.
soccer.guide.five-v-five.step3.title: Add a Condition
soccer.guide.five-v-five.step3.descripti: Introduce a condition such as 'three passes before shooting' or 'must score from inside the scoring zone'. This forces players to apply the skills from earlier drills.
soccer.guide.five-v-five.step4.title: Debrief and Repeat
soccer.guide.five-v-five.step4.descripti: Pause the game briefly to discuss what is working and what is not. Restart for a final round and challenge teams to apply the feedback.
soccer.guide.five-v-five.tip1: Keep the game moving. If the ball goes out, have spare balls ready at the sideline to restart quickly
soccer.guide.five-v-five.tip2: Praise players who move into space without the ball, not just the goal scorers

volleyball.drill.pepper.name: Pepper
volleyball.drill.pepper.description: Partner drill: bump, set, hit in a continuous rally.
volleyball.drill.platform-passing.name: Platform Passing Lines
volleyball.drill.platform-passing.descri: Lines of players practice forearm passing technique.
volleyball.drill.serve-receive.name: Serve Receive Formation
volleyball.drill.serve-receive.descripti: Practice serve receive positions and passing to the setter.
volleyball.drill.setting-target.name: Setting Target Practice
volleyball.drill.setting-target.descript: Setters practice accuracy by setting to specific targets.
volleyball.drill.approach-hit.name: Approach & Hit
volleyball.drill.approach-hit.descriptio: Practice the 4-step approach and attack from different positions.
volleyball.drill.cross-line-shot.name: Cross-Court vs Line Shot
volleyball.drill.cross-line-shot.descrip: Hitters practice directing their attacks cross-court or down the line.
volleyball.drill.block-timing.name: Block Timing Drill
volleyball.drill.block-timing.descriptio: Practice timing the block jump with the attacker's approach.
volleyball.drill.overhand-serve.name: Overhand Serve Progression
volleyball.drill.overhand-serve.descript: Step-by-step progression to learn the overhand serve.
volleyball.drill.dig-transition.name: Dig and Transition
volleyball.drill.dig-transition.descript: Dig an attack and transition to offense.
volleyball.drill.rotation-practice.name: 6v6 Rotation Practice
volleyball.drill.rotation-practice.descr: Full team rotation practice with serve receive and attack.
volleyball.guide.pepper.step1.title: Pair Up and Space Out
volleyball.guide.pepper.step1.descriptio: Players form pairs and stand about 3-4 meters apart facing each other. Each pair has one ball.
volleyball.guide.pepper.step2.title: Start the Bump-Set-Hit Cycle
volleyball.guide.pepper.step2.descriptio: Player A bumps the ball to Player B. Player B sets it back. Player A hits it down gently toward Player B. Then reverse roles. Keep the rally going.
volleyball.guide.pepper.step3.title: Increase Tempo
volleyball.guide.pepper.step3.descriptio: Once pairs are comfortable, gradually increase the speed. Challenge each pair to keep a rally going for 10 or more contacts without letting the ball drop.
volleyball.guide.pepper.tip1: Keep the ball controlled - this is a warm-up, not a competition to hit hard
volleyball.guide.pepper.tip2: Bend your knees and stay light on your feet to react quickly
Split players into two lines facing each other across the court, about 5 meters apart. The front player of one line has the ball.
The player with the ball tosses it underhand to the player opposite. That player uses a forearm pass (bump) to send it back. After passing, move to the end of your line.
Advance to continuous passing where each player bumps and immediately rotates to the back of the line. Keep the ball moving between the two lines without pauses.
volleyball.guide.platform-passing.tip1: Lock your elbows and create a flat platform with your forearms - do not swing your arms
volleyball.guide.platform-passing.tip2: Move your feet to get behind the ball instead of reaching with your arms
volleyball.guide.serve-receive.step1.des: Place 3 receivers in a W-formation on one side of the net. Position a setter near the net at position 2/3. Servers line up on the opposite side behind the end line.
volleyball.guide.serve-receive.step2.des: A server delivers an underhand or overhand serve. The designated receiver calls the ball and passes it to the setter target at the net.
volleyball.guide.serve-receive.step3.des: Emphasize calling 'mine' loudly before receiving. Non-receivers open up to support. The setter calls for the ball to give the passer a target.
volleyball.guide.serve-receive.step4.des: After every 3 serves, rotate the receivers so each player practices from all positions in the formation. Servers also rotate in.
volleyball.guide.serve-receive.tip1: Angle your platform toward the setter target - do not just bump the ball straight up
volleyball.guide.serve-receive.tip2: Stay on the balls of your feet with knees bent so you can move quickly in any direction
volleyball.guide.setting-target.step1.de: Set up targets (cones or hoops) at the outside hitter position (position 4) and right-side position (position 2) near the net. The setter stands at position 2/3.
volleyball.guide.setting-target.step2.de: A coach or player tosses the ball to the setter, who uses an overhead set to direct the ball to the target on the left side. Focus on a high, accurate arc.
volleyball.guide.setting-target.step3.de: After 5 sets to the left, switch to the right-side target. The coach can call out 'left' or 'right' just before the toss to practice quick decision-making.
volleyball.guide.setting-target.tip1: Use your legs to power the set - push up from your knees rather than just using your wrists
volleyball.guide.setting-target.tip2: Get directly under the ball with your hands shaped like a triangle above your forehead
volleyball.guide.approach-hit.step1.desc: Show the 4-step approach: right-left-right-left (for right-handers). Walk through it slowly, then at half speed. Have all players practice the steps without a ball.
volleyball.guide.approach-hit.step2.desc: Players line up at position 4. Each player performs the 4-step approach and jumps, reaching high with the hitting arm. No ball yet - focus on timing and a strong arm swing.
volleyball.guide.approach-hit.step3.desc: A setter or coach stands at the net and tosses the ball. The hitter performs the full approach and hits the tossed ball over the net. Focus on contacting the ball at the highest point.
volleyball.guide.approach-hit.step4.desc: A passer feeds the setter, who sets to position 4. The hitter runs the full approach and attacks a live set. Rotate through all hitters.
volleyball.guide.approach-hit.tip1: The last two steps (right-left) should be quick and explosive like a brake-and-jump
volleyball.guide.approach-hit.tip2: Swing your arms back on the last step and drive them up to gain height on the jump
volleyball.guide.cross-line-shot.step1.d: Show players the two main attack angles: cross-court (diagonal) and down the line (straight along the sideline). Mark target zones on the opposite court with cones.
volleyball.guide.cross-line-shot.step2.d: Hitters approach from position 4 and hit cross-court toward the cones in zone 5. The setter provides a high outside set. Each player gets 5 attempts.
volleyball.guide.cross-line-shot.step3.d: Same setup, but now hitters aim down the line toward cones in zone 1. Emphasize turning the wrist and contacting the ball on the right side to direct it down the line.
volleyball.guide.cross-line-shot.step4.d: The coach calls 'cross' or 'line' as the hitter begins their approach. This trains quick decision-making and the ability to adjust mid-approach.
volleyball.guide.cross-line-shot.tip1: For cross-court, open your hips and shoulders toward the opposite corner as you swing
volleyball.guide.cross-line-shot.tip2: For line shots, keep your shoulder square to the net and snap your wrist straight ahead
volleyball.guide.block-timing.step1.desc: Blockers start at the center of the net. Practice shuffle-stepping to position 4, jumping with hands over the net, then shuffling back to center and repeating to position 2.
volleyball.guide.block-timing.step2.desc: An attacker on the other side performs their approach without hitting. The blocker watches the attacker's shoulder and jumps when the attacker jumps. Practice timing 10 times.
volleyball.guide.block-timing.step3.desc: A setter sets to an attacker. The blocker reads the set, shuffles into position, and jumps to block. Start with predictable sets, then mix up left and right.
volleyball.guide.block-timing.step4.desc: Add a second blocker. The middle blocker reads the set and closes to the outside blocker. Both jump together with hands sealed. Practice closing the gap between the blockers.
volleyball.guide.block-timing.tip1: Keep your hands wide and firm with fingers spread - do not pull your hands back on contact
volleyball.guide.block-timing.tip2: Jump slightly after the attacker - if you jump at the same time you will come down too early
volleyball.guide.overhand-serve.step1.de: Stand behind the end line. Hold the ball in your non-hitting hand. Practice tossing the ball about 1 meter above your head, slightly in front of your hitting shoulder. Do 10 tosses without hitting.
volleyball.guide.overhand-serve.step2.de: Toss the ball and hit it with an open hand over the net from the service line (not the end line). Focus on contacting the ball with the heel of your hand at full arm extension.
volleyball.guide.overhand-serve.step3.de: Once players can consistently get the ball over from the service line, move back 1 meter at a time. Emphasize using the whole body - step, rotate hips, then swing.
volleyball.guide.overhand-serve.step4.de: Players serve from behind the end line. Aim for the middle of the opposite court first. Once consistent, aim for specific zones (short, deep, left, right).
volleyball.guide.overhand-serve.tip1: A consistent toss is the most important part - if the toss is bad, catch it and start over
volleyball.guide.overhand-serve.tip2: Contact the ball with a stiff wrist and follow through toward your target
volleyball.guide.dig-transition.step1.de: Place 3 defenders on one side of the net in base defensive positions (positions 1, 5, and 6). A coach or attacker stands on a box on the other side of the net.
volleyball.guide.dig-transition.step2.de: The coach hits or tosses the ball at the defenders. The nearest defender digs the ball up and toward the setter position. Focus on absorbing the ball with a low platform.
volleyball.guide.dig-transition.step3.de: After the dig, the setter moves to the ball and sets to an attacker. The other defenders transition from defense to hitting positions. Run the full dig-set-hit sequence.
volleyball.guide.dig-transition.step4.de: The coach sends another ball immediately after each attack. The defenders must quickly reset to defensive position and dig again. Do 5 continuous rallies per group.
volleyball.guide.dig-transition.tip1: Stay low in your ready position with weight forward - do not stand upright between plays
volleyball.guide.dig-transition.tip2: After the dig, immediately look for where to transition - do not watch your own pass
volleyball.guide.rotation-practice.step1: Split into two teams of 6. Assign each player a position number (1-6). Explain the rotation order and where each position stands for serve receive.
volleyball.guide.rotation-practice.step2: Without a ball, walk through each of the 6 rotations. After each side-out, the team rotates clockwise. Make sure every player knows their spot in every rotation.
volleyball.guide.rotation-practice.step3: Play out a rally starting with a serve. The receiving team passes, sets, and attacks. After the rally, the winning team serves and the other team rotates. Play 3 rallies per rotation.
volleyball.guide.rotation-practice.step4: Play a game to 15 points. Pause after each rotation to check that all players are in the correct position before the serve. Correct any overlap violations.
volleyball.guide.rotation-practice.step5: Gather the team. Call out a rotation number and have players run to their correct positions. Repeat for all 6 rotations until everyone moves quickly and confidently.
volleyball.guide.rotation-practice.tip1: Use colored pinnies or numbered jerseys so players can easily see who should be where
volleyball.guide.rotation-practice.tip2: Remind players that overlap rules only apply at the moment of serve - after that they can move freely
volleyball.anim.pepper.phase1.name: Setup
volleyball.anim.pepper.phase1.descriptio: Two partners face each other about 3-4 meters apart with one ball.
volleyball.anim.pepper.phase2.name: Bump-Set-Hit Cycle
volleyball.anim.pepper.phase2.descriptio: Player A bumps to B, B sets back, A hits down gently. Then roles reverse.
volleyball.anim.pepper.phase3.name: Continuous Rally
volleyball.anim.pepper.phase3.descriptio: Partners increase tempo and maintain a continuous bump-set-hit rally.
The target player tosses the ball toward the first passer in line.
The passer uses a forearm platform pass to send the ball back to the target.
The passer rotates to the back of the line and the next passer steps up.
volleyball.anim.serve-receive.phase1.des: The server delivers the ball over the net toward the receivers in W-formation.
volleyball.anim.serve-receive.phase2.des: The receiver calls the ball, platforms it toward the setter at the net.
volleyball.anim.serve-receive.phase3.des: The setter catches or sets the ball. Receivers rotate positions for the next serve.
volleyball.anim.setting-target.phase1.de: The coach tosses the ball to the setter standing near the net.
volleyball.anim.setting-target.phase2.de: The setter overhead-sets the ball to the target cone at the outside hitter position.
volleyball.anim.setting-target.phase3.de: On the next repetition the setter directs the ball to the right-side target cone.
volleyball.anim.approach-hit.phase1.name: Pass to Setter
volleyball.anim.approach-hit.phase1.desc: The passer sends the ball to the setter near the net while the hitter prepares.
volleyball.anim.approach-hit.phase2.name: Set and Approach
volleyball.anim.approach-hit.phase2.desc: The setter delivers a high outside set. The hitter begins the 4-step approach toward the net.
volleyball.anim.approach-hit.phase3.name: Jump and Spike
volleyball.anim.approach-hit.phase3.desc: The hitter jumps at the net and spikes the ball over to the opposite court.
volleyball.anim.cross-line-shot.phase1.d: The setter delivers a high outside set as the hitter begins the approach.
volleyball.anim.cross-line-shot.phase2.d: The hitter opens the hips and hits diagonally toward the cross-court target cone.
volleyball.anim.cross-line-shot.phase3.d: On the next rep the hitter keeps the shoulder square and hits straight down the line.
volleyball.anim.block-timing.phase1.name: Set to Attacker
volleyball.anim.block-timing.phase1.desc: The setter on the attacking side delivers the ball to the outside hitter.
volleyball.anim.block-timing.phase2.name: Read and Jump
volleyball.anim.block-timing.phase2.desc: The blocker reads the set, shuffles into position, and times the jump with the attacker.
volleyball.anim.block-timing.phase3.name: Block and Reset
volleyball.anim.block-timing.phase3.desc: The blocker presses hands over the net to deflect the ball, then resets to base position.
volleyball.anim.dig-transition.phase1.de: The coach on the box hits the ball at the defenders in base defensive positions.
volleyball.anim.dig-transition.phase2.de: A defender digs the ball up to the setter, who sets an attacker transitioning from defense.
volleyball.anim.dig-transition.phase3.de: The attacker spikes the ball over the net while defenders reset for the next rally.
volleyball.anim.rotation-practice.phase1: The team is in starting rotation. Player 1 serves the ball over the net.
volleyball.anim.rotation-practice.phase2: After winning the rally the team rotates one position clockwise.
volleyball.anim.rotation-practice.phase3: The new server serves from position 1 and play continues in the new rotation.
handball.drill.passing-circle.name: Passing Circle on the Move
handball.drill.passing-circle.descriptio: Players pass while moving in a circle, improving catching on the run.
handball.drill.cross-pass.name: Cross Pass Drill
handball.drill.cross-pass.description: Two lines cross paths, passing the ball mid-run.
handball.drill.jump-shot.name: Jump Shot Technique
handball.drill.jump-shot.description: Practice the jump shot with proper footwork and release.
handball.drill.wing-shot.name: Wing Shot Drill
handball.drill.wing-shot.description: Shooting from the wing position with a tight angle.
handball.drill.fast-break.name: Fast Break 3-on-2
handball.drill.fast-break.description: Three attackers against two defenders in a fast break scenario.
handball.drill.crisscross.name: Crisscross (X-Play)
handball.drill.crisscross.description: Two players cross paths to confuse the defense and create space.
handball.drill.defense-6-0.name: 6-0 Defense Formation
handball.drill.defense-6-0.description: Practice the flat 6-0 defensive setup with all defenders on the 6m line.
handball.drill.defense-5-1.name: 5-1 Defense Transition
handball.drill.defense-5-1.description: Practice the 5-1 defense with one advanced defender.
handball.drill.goalkeeper-reflex.name: Goalkeeper Reflex Drill
handball.drill.goalkeeper-reflex.descrip: Quick reaction saves from close range shots.
handball.drill.half-court-5v5.name: Half-Court 5v5 Play
handball.drill.half-court-5v5.descriptio: Half-court game focusing on attack patterns and defensive positioning.
handball.guide.passing-circle.step1.desc: All players form a circle with about 3 meters between each person. Start jogging slowly in the same direction around the circle.
handball.guide.passing-circle.step2.desc: Add one ball. Players pass across the circle to a player on the opposite side while continuing to jog. Use chest passes with two hands.
handball.guide.passing-circle.step3.desc: Introduce a second ball so two passes happen simultaneously. Players must keep their heads up and communicate before passing.
handball.guide.passing-circle.step4.desc: On the coach's whistle, reverse the jogging direction. Increase the pace gradually. Challenge players to use one-handed passes.
handball.guide.passing-circle.tip1: Remind players to call the name of the receiver before passing
handball.guide.passing-circle.tip2: Encourage soft hands when catching - pull the ball into the body
handball.guide.cross-pass.step1.title: Set Up Two Lines
handball.guide.cross-pass.step1.descript: Place two lines of players at opposite corners of the half court. Each line faces the diagonally opposite corner.
handball.guide.cross-pass.step2.title: Cross and Pass
handball.guide.cross-pass.step2.descript: The first player from each line jogs toward the center. As they cross paths, the player with the ball passes to the other. Both continue to the opposite corner.
handball.guide.cross-pass.step3.title: Increase Tempo and Add Defenders
handball.guide.cross-pass.step3.descript: Speed up the runs to game pace. Optionally add a passive defender at the crossing point so players must time the pass to avoid interception.
handball.guide.cross-pass.tip1: Pass the ball at the exact moment of crossing, not before or after
handball.guide.cross-pass.tip2: Receivers should show a target hand to guide the passer
handball.guide.jump-shot.step1.title: Demonstrate the Footwork
handball.guide.jump-shot.step1.descripti: Show the three-step approach: left-right-left for right-handers (right-left-right for left-handers). Practice the steps without a ball first.
handball.guide.jump-shot.step2.title: Add the Jump
handball.guide.jump-shot.step2.descripti: Players perform the three-step approach and jump off the lead foot. Focus on gaining height and hanging in the air. No ball yet - just mimic the throwing motion.
handball.guide.jump-shot.step3.title: Add the Ball
handball.guide.jump-shot.step3.descripti: Now with a ball, players approach from the 9-meter line, jump, and throw at the goal. Focus on a high arm release and wrist snap.
handball.guide.jump-shot.step4.title: Shoot Against a Goalkeeper
handball.guide.jump-shot.step4.descripti: Add a goalkeeper. Players take turns shooting from different angles along the 9-meter line. Aim for corners and vary shot placement.
handball.guide.jump-shot.tip1: The throwing arm should be fully extended above the head at release
handball.guide.jump-shot.tip2: Land on both feet with bent knees to protect joints
handball.guide.wing-shot.step1.title: Explain the Wing Position
handball.guide.wing-shot.step1.descripti: Show the wing area on both sides of the court. Explain the tight angle and why body positioning matters for wing shots.
handball.guide.wing-shot.step2.title: Practice the Approach
handball.guide.wing-shot.step2.descripti: Players run along the sideline toward the goal, receive a pass from the backcourt, and practice the footwork to get into a shooting position at a steep angle.
handball.guide.wing-shot.step3.title: Shoot at Goal
handball.guide.wing-shot.step3.descripti: Players now shoot at goal from the wing. Focus on shooting across the goalkeeper to the far post and using body lean to open the angle. Alternate left and right wings.
handball.guide.wing-shot.tip1: Lean your upper body away from the goal to create a wider shooting angle
handball.guide.wing-shot.tip2: Aim for the far corner - the near post is the goalkeeper's strongest side from the wing
handball.guide.fast-break.step1.title: Set Up the Scenario
handball.guide.fast-break.step1.descript: Position three attackers at the center line and two defenders near the 9-meter line. The goalkeeper starts in goal.
handball.guide.fast-break.step2.title: Launch the Break
handball.guide.fast-break.step2.descript: On the whistle, the center attacker passes to either wing and all three sprint toward goal. The two defenders backpedal to cover. Attackers must move the ball quickly.
handball.guide.fast-break.step3.title: Create the Overload
handball.guide.fast-break.step3.descript: The ball carrier drives at one defender to commit them, then passes to the free player. Practice reading which side has the 2-on-1 advantage.
handball.guide.fast-break.step4.title: Finish and Rotate
handball.guide.fast-break.step4.descript: Finish with a shot on goal. Rotate players so everyone plays as attacker and defender. Discuss what worked after each round.
handball.guide.fast-break.tip1: The ball should move faster than the players - pass early, don't dribble
handball.guide.fast-break.tip2: Attackers should spread wide to stretch the two defenders
handball.guide.crisscross.step1.title: Explain the X-Play Concept
handball.guide.crisscross.step1.descript: Two backcourt players stand side by side near the 9-meter line. Explain that they will cross paths to confuse defenders and create a gap.
handball.guide.crisscross.step2.title: Walk Through the Crossing
handball.guide.crisscross.step2.descript: At walking speed, the ball carrier moves diagonally across while handing off or passing to the crossing partner running the opposite direction. Practice the timing.
handball.guide.crisscross.step3.title: Add Speed and Defenders
handball.guide.crisscross.step3.descript: Increase to game speed. Add two passive defenders at the 6-meter line so the crossing players must read the gap and decide who takes the shot.
handball.guide.crisscross.step4.title: Full Integration
handball.guide.crisscross.step4.descript: Run the crisscross as part of a half-court attack with other players (pivot, wings). The crossers decide to shoot, pass to the pivot, or pass to the wing.
handball.guide.crisscross.tip1: The crossing must be tight - players should almost brush shoulders to confuse the defense
handball.guide.crisscross.tip2: The ball carrier should fake a shot before the handoff to freeze the defender
handball.guide.defense-6-0.step1.title: Line Up on the 6-Meter Line
handball.guide.defense-6-0.step1.descrip: Six defenders stand shoulder to shoulder along the 6-meter line. Explain that in 6-0 defense, nobody steps forward - the wall stays flat.
handball.guide.defense-6-0.step2.title: Practice Lateral Shifting
handball.guide.defense-6-0.step2.descrip: An attacker passes the ball along the 9-meter line. All six defenders shift together as a unit toward the ball side, maintaining their line.
handball.guide.defense-6-0.step3.title: Close Gaps on Penetration
handball.guide.defense-6-0.step3.descrip: Add attackers who try to drive into gaps. Defenders must close together when an attacker approaches and push them out. Practice squeezing the gap.
handball.guide.defense-6-0.step4.title: Full 6-on-6 Defense
handball.guide.defense-6-0.step4.descrip: Run a full 6-on-6 scenario. The offense tries to break through the 6-0 wall while the defense shifts, blocks, and communicates.
handball.guide.defense-6-0.tip1: Defenders must talk constantly - call out who they are covering
handball.guide.defense-6-0.tip2: Keep arms up and active to block passing lanes and shots
handball.guide.defense-5-1.step1.title: Explain the 5-1 Formation
handball.guide.defense-5-1.step1.descrip: Five defenders line up on the 6-meter line while one advanced defender steps out to the 9-meter area. Explain the advanced player's role: disrupting the center backcourt.
handball.guide.defense-5-1.step2.title: Practice the Advanced Defender's Movement
handball.guide.defense-5-1.step2.descrip: The advanced defender practices pressuring the ball handler at 9 meters, then retreating quickly when the ball is passed. Emphasize quick feet and reading the play.
handball.guide.defense-5-1.step3.title: Coordinate the Back Five
handball.guide.defense-5-1.step3.descrip: The remaining five defenders practice shifting to cover the gap left by the advanced player. When the advanced defender steps out, the two nearest defenders close the center.
handball.guide.defense-5-1.step4.title: Live 6-on-6 Practice
handball.guide.defense-5-1.step4.descrip: Run a full 6-on-6 half-court game. The defense uses 5-1 while the offense tries to exploit the gap left by the advanced defender. Rotate the advanced defender role.
handball.guide.defense-5-1.tip1: The advanced defender should only press when the center back has the ball
handball.guide.defense-5-1.tip2: Communication between the advanced player and the back five is critical - use short calls
handball.guide.goalkeeper-reflex.step1.d: The goalkeeper stands in the center of the goal. A partner rolls balls along the ground alternating left and right. The keeper drops to save each ball. 10 rolls each side.
handball.guide.goalkeeper-reflex.step2.d: A shooter stands at the 7-meter mark and throws at different corners in quick succession (one throw every 3 seconds). The goalkeeper reacts and resets position after each save.
handball.guide.goalkeeper-reflex.step3.d: Place 2-3 shooters at different positions around the 9-meter line. They take turns shooting quickly so the goalkeeper must adjust position and react from different angles.
handball.guide.goalkeeper-reflex.tip1: The goalkeeper should always return to the center of the goal between shots
handball.guide.goalkeeper-reflex.tip2: Focus on staying on the balls of the feet - flat feet slow reaction time
handball.guide.half-court-5v5.step1.desc: Split into two teams of 5 field players each (plus goalkeepers). Use bibs to distinguish teams. Play on one half of the court only. After a goal, the defending team restarts at the center line.
handball.guide.half-court-5v5.step2.desc: Start with a condition: every player must touch the ball before the team can shoot. This encourages passing and movement. Play 3-minute rounds.
handball.guide.half-court-5v5.step3.desc: Remove the passing condition and let teams play freely. Coach observes and gives brief feedback between rounds. Switch matchups by rotating one team's players.
handball.guide.half-court-5v5.step4.desc: Pause the game when you see a teachable moment. Freeze the players and show better positioning, passing options, or defensive shape. Then resume play.
handball.guide.half-court-5v5.tip1: Encourage players to use the full width of the court, not just the center
handball.guide.half-court-5v5.tip2: Rotate goalkeepers every few rounds so everyone gets court time
handball.anim.passing-circle.phase1.name: Form the Circle
handball.anim.passing-circle.phase1.desc: Players form a circle and begin jogging clockwise together.
handball.anim.passing-circle.phase2.name: Pass Across
handball.anim.passing-circle.phase2.desc: Players pass the ball across the circle to the opposite side while continuing to jog.
handball.anim.passing-circle.phase3.name: Speed Up and Reverse
handball.anim.passing-circle.phase3.desc: The circle reverses direction. Passing tempo increases with quicker releases.
handball.anim.cross-pass.phase1.name: Line Up
handball.anim.cross-pass.phase1.descript: Two players start at opposite corners and begin jogging toward the center.
handball.anim.cross-pass.phase2.name: Cross and Exchange
handball.anim.cross-pass.phase2.descript: Players cross paths at the center and the ball carrier passes to the other player mid-run.
handball.anim.cross-pass.phase3.name: Continue to Corners
handball.anim.cross-pass.phase3.descript: Both players finish their runs to the opposite corners. The next pair prepares to go.
handball.anim.jump-shot.phase1.name: Receive and Approach
handball.anim.jump-shot.phase1.descripti: The shooter receives a pass from the feeder and begins the three-step approach toward the goal.
handball.anim.jump-shot.phase2.name: Jump and Arm Back
handball.anim.jump-shot.phase2.descripti: The shooter plants the lead foot, jumps high into the air, and brings the throwing arm back.
handball.anim.jump-shot.phase3.name: Release and Follow Through
handball.anim.jump-shot.phase3.descripti: The ball is released at the highest point with a wrist snap, aimed at the far corner of the goal.
handball.anim.wing-shot.phase1.name: Wing Run and Pass
handball.anim.wing-shot.phase1.descripti: The wing player sprints along the sideline. The center back carries the ball and prepares to pass.
handball.anim.wing-shot.phase2.name: Receive on the Wing
handball.anim.wing-shot.phase2.descripti: The wing receives the pass near the 6-meter line at a steep angle and adjusts body position.
handball.anim.wing-shot.phase3.name: Shoot Across Goal
handball.anim.wing-shot.phase3.descripti: The wing player leans away to widen the angle and shoots across the goalkeeper to the far post.
handball.anim.fast-break.phase1.name: Outlet Pass
handball.anim.fast-break.phase1.descript: Three attackers line up at center court. The center player passes to a wing to start the fast break.
handball.anim.fast-break.phase2.name: Sprint and Overlap
handball.anim.fast-break.phase2.descript: All three attackers sprint forward, moving the ball quickly. Two defenders backpedal to cover.
handball.anim.fast-break.phase3.name: Exploit the Overload
handball.anim.fast-break.phase3.descript: The ball carrier commits a defender and passes to the free player for a shot on goal.
handball.anim.crisscross.phase1.name: Set Up the Cross
handball.anim.crisscross.phase1.descript: Two backcourt players stand side by side at the 9-meter line. The ball carrier begins moving diagonally.
handball.anim.crisscross.phase2.name: Exchange and Cross
handball.anim.crisscross.phase2.descript: The players cross paths tightly, exchanging the ball at the moment of crossing to confuse the defenders.
handball.anim.crisscross.phase3.name: Attack the Gap
handball.anim.crisscross.phase3.descript: The new ball carrier drives into the gap created by the crossing movement and takes a shot.
handball.anim.defense-6-0.phase1.name: Set the Wall
handball.anim.defense-6-0.phase1.descrip: Six defenders line up shoulder to shoulder on the 6-meter line forming a flat wall.
handball.anim.defense-6-0.phase2.name: Shift as a Unit
handball.anim.defense-6-0.phase2.descrip: As the attacker moves the ball to the side, all six defenders shift together to stay between ball and goal.
handball.anim.defense-6-0.phase3.name: Close the Gap
handball.anim.defense-6-0.phase3.descrip: When the attacker drives toward the line, the nearest defenders squeeze together to block the penetration.
handball.anim.defense-5-1.phase1.name: Formation Setup
handball.anim.defense-5-1.phase1.descrip: Five defenders hold the 6-meter line while one advanced defender steps out to the 9-meter area.
handball.anim.defense-5-1.phase2.name: Press and Retreat
handball.anim.defense-5-1.phase2.descrip: The advanced defender pressures the ball handler, then retreats when the ball is passed to the side.
handball.anim.defense-5-1.phase3.name: Cover the Gap
handball.anim.defense-5-1.phase3.descrip: The back five shift to cover the space left by the advanced defender. The attacker tries to exploit the center.
basketball.guide.dynamic-stretching.step: All players form a large circle on the court with about 2 meters between each player.
Start with small arm circles, gradually making them larger. 15 seconds forward, 15 seconds backward.
Hold a partner's shoulder for balance. Swing one leg forward and back, 10 times each leg.
Jog in place bringing knees up to hip height. 20 seconds.
Take a big step forward into a lunge, twist your torso toward the front knee. Alternate legs for 10 reps.
basketball.guide.zigzag-dribble.step1.de: Place 8-10 cones in a zigzag pattern, about 2 meters apart.
basketball.guide.zigzag-dribble.step2.de: Dribble through the cones using only your right hand. Focus on keeping the ball low and controlled.
basketball.guide.zigzag-dribble.step3.de: Repeat the course using only your left hand.
basketball.guide.zigzag-dribble.step4.de: Switch hands with a crossover dribble at each cone.
basketball.guide.zigzag-dribble.tip1: Keep your head up, don't look at the ball
basketball.guide.zigzag-dribble.tip2: Stay low in an athletic stance
basketball.guide.layup-lines.step1.title: Form Two Lines
basketball.guide.layup-lines.step1.descr: One line at the right wing (shooting line) and one at the left wing (rebounding line).
basketball.guide.layup-lines.step2.title: Dribble and Shoot
basketball.guide.layup-lines.step2.descr: First player in the shooting line dribbles toward the basket and performs a right-hand layup.
basketball.guide.layup-lines.step3.title: Rebound
basketball.guide.layup-lines.step3.descr: First player in the rebounding line grabs the rebound and passes to the next shooter.
basketball.guide.layup-lines.step4.title: Switch Sides
basketball.guide.layup-lines.step4.descr: After shooting, go to the rebounding line. After rebounding, go to the shooting line. Switch to left side after 5 minutes.
basketball.guide.layup-lines.tip1: Use the backboard - aim for the top corner of the square
basketball.guide.layup-lines.tip2: Right hand layup from right side, left hand from left side
basketball.guide.sharks-minnows.step1.de: Use the full court or half court. All minnows (dribblers) line up on one baseline, each with a ball. Select 1-2 players as sharks who stand in the center circle without a ball.
basketball.guide.sharks-minnows.step2.de: On the whistle, minnows must dribble to the opposite baseline without losing their ball. Sharks try to knock the ball away. If a minnow loses their ball, they become a shark for the next round.
basketball.guide.sharks-minnows.step3.de: Blow the whistle to start each round. As more players become sharks, the game gets harder. Continue until only 1-2 minnows remain. They become the sharks for the next game.
basketball.guide.sharks-minnows.step4.de: Challenge players by requiring weak-hand only dribbling, or narrow the playing area with cones to increase difficulty.
basketball.guide.sharks-minnows.tip1: Remind sharks they can only knock the ball away, no holding or body contact
basketball.guide.sharks-minnows.tip2: Encourage dribblers to use their body to shield the ball and change speed
basketball.guide.chest-bounce-pass.step1: Players form pairs facing each other, about 3-4 meters apart. Each pair has one ball.
basketball.guide.chest-bounce-pass.step2: Hold the ball at chest height with both hands. Step forward and push the ball straight to your partner's chest. Thumbs should point down after release. Do 15 chest passes each.
basketball.guide.chest-bounce-pass.step3: Same starting position. Push the ball toward the floor about two-thirds of the way to your partner. The ball should bounce up to their waist. Do 15 bounce passes each.
basketball.guide.chest-bounce-pass.step4: Alternate between chest and bounce passes at increasing speed. The coach calls out which type to use. Gradually increase the distance between partners.
basketball.guide.chest-bounce-pass.tip1: Step into the pass for power - the force comes from the legs, not just the arms
basketball.guide.chest-bounce-pass.tip2: Receiving hands should be out as a target, fingers spread with thumbs up
basketball.guide.three-person-weave.step: Three players line up on the baseline: left wing, center, right wing. The center has the ball. After passing, you always run behind the person you passed to.
Walk through the pattern slowly. Center passes to the right wing and runs behind them. Right wing passes to the left wing and runs behind. Continue this weaving pattern down the court.
Now run the weave at full speed. The ball should not touch the floor. Finish with a layup at the far basket. Rotate groups so everyone gets repetitions.
After the layup, the rebounder outlets the ball and the same three players weave back to the starting basket for another layup.
basketball.guide.three-person-weave.tip1: Pass the ball ahead of the runner so they catch it in stride, not behind them
basketball.guide.three-person-weave.tip2: Communicate loudly - call for the ball when you are open
basketball.guide.free-throw.step1.title: Teach the Routine
basketball.guide.free-throw.step1.descri: Each player stands at the free throw line. Demonstrate a consistent routine: bounce the ball 2-3 times, take a breath, bend knees, and shoot. Have every player establish their own rhythm.
basketball.guide.free-throw.step2.title: Focus on Form
basketball.guide.free-throw.step2.descri: Players shoot in pairs (one shoots, one rebounds). Focus on keeping the elbow under the ball, a high follow-through, and a soft backspin. Each player shoots 10 free throws.
basketball.guide.free-throw.step3.title: Pressure Shooting
basketball.guide.free-throw.step3.descri: Add game pressure: players must make 2 in a row before rotating out. Or run a team challenge where the group must reach a total of 20 makes. Add consequences like a light jog for misses.
basketball.guide.free-throw.tip1: Bend your knees - most missed free throws are short because of lazy legs
basketball.guide.free-throw.tip2: Keep the same routine every single time, even during games
basketball.guide.give-and-go.step1.title: Demonstrate the Move
basketball.guide.give-and-go.step1.descr: Show the basic give-and-go: Player A passes to Player B at the wing, then immediately cuts hard to the basket. Player B passes back to A for the layup. Walk through it once.
basketball.guide.give-and-go.step2.title: Practice Without Defense
basketball.guide.give-and-go.step2.descr: Pairs practice the give-and-go from the top of the key. Focus on a sharp, decisive cut after the pass. No standing and watching - the cut must happen immediately.
basketball.guide.give-and-go.step3.title: Add a Passive Defender
basketball.guide.give-and-go.step3.descr: Add a defender who plays at 50% effort. The cutter must read the defender and decide to cut baseline or to the middle. The passer must deliver the ball with correct timing.
basketball.guide.give-and-go.step4.title: Live Play
basketball.guide.give-and-go.step4.descr: Play 2-on-2 where teams score extra points for successfully completing a give-and-go. Rotate offense and defense every 3 possessions.
basketball.guide.give-and-go.tip1: The cut must be explosive - change speed to lose the defender
basketball.guide.give-and-go.tip2: The passer should fake a second action (jab step or look away) before the return pass
basketball.guide.pick-and-roll.step1.des: Pair up players. One is the ball handler, the other is the screener. Explain that the screener sets a solid screen on the ball handler's defender, then rolls to the basket.
basketball.guide.pick-and-roll.step2.des: Without defense, the screener runs to the ball handler and sets a wide, stable screen with feet shoulder-width apart and arms crossed over the chest. The ball handler dribbles off the screen.
basketball.guide.pick-and-roll.step3.des: After the ball handler uses the screen, the screener pivots toward the basket (the roll) and receives a pass for a layup. Add a passive defender on the ball handler to make it realistic.
basketball.guide.pick-and-roll.step4.des: Play 2-on-2 using the pick and roll. The ball handler reads the defense: if the defender goes under the screen, shoot. If they go over, drive. If they switch, pass to the roller.
basketball.guide.pick-and-roll.tip1: The screener must be set and stationary before contact - moving screens are fouls
basketball.guide.pick-and-roll.tip2: The ball handler should wait for the screen to be set before driving, do not rush
Place cones in a straight line across the court about 3 meters apart. Players line up at one end in defensive stance: knees bent, feet wide, hands active.
Players slide laterally from cone to cone. Push off the trailing foot, never cross your feet. Touch each cone and change direction. Go for 30 seconds, rest 15 seconds, repeat 3 times.
Now slide diagonally forward to one cone, then diagonally back to the next, creating a zig-zag pattern. This simulates staying in front of an offensive player changing direction.
Pair up. One player is the offensive player with a ball, the other mirrors their movements using defensive slides. The offensive player changes direction every 2-3 seconds. Switch after 30 seconds.
basketball.guide.defensive-slides.tip1: Stay low - if you stand up, you lose quickness and balance
basketball.guide.defensive-slides.tip2: Keep your chest facing the offensive player and never turn your hips sideways
basketball.guide.shell-drill.step1.title: Set Up 4-on-4
basketball.guide.shell-drill.step1.descr: Place 4 offensive players around the three-point line (two wings, two slots). Match up 4 defenders. Offense only passes the ball, they do not dribble or shoot yet.
basketball.guide.shell-drill.step2.title: Ball-Side and Help-Side Positioning
basketball.guide.shell-drill.step2.descr: As the ball moves around the perimeter, defenders adjust. On-ball defender pressures the ball. Defenders one pass away deny the pass. Help-side defenders sag toward the lane with one foot in the paint.
basketball.guide.shell-drill.step3.title: Add Basket Cuts
basketball.guide.shell-drill.step3.descr: Allow offensive players to cut to the basket. Defenders must jump to the ball on every pass and stay between their player and the basket. If beaten on a cut, help-side defense must rotate.
basketball.guide.shell-drill.step4.title: Add Dribble Penetration
basketball.guide.shell-drill.step4.descr: Now the ball handler can attack with one or two dribbles. The nearest help defender must stop the drive, and all other defenders rotate to cover the open player.
basketball.guide.shell-drill.step5.title: Full Live Play
basketball.guide.shell-drill.step5.descr: Allow offense to play freely. Defense must communicate and apply all the principles. Play to a stop (defensive rebound, steal, or score), then rotate offense to defense.
basketball.guide.shell-drill.tip1: Constant talking is key - defenders must call out screens, cuts, and help
basketball.guide.shell-drill.tip2: Freeze the drill when positioning breaks down and correct before continuing
basketball.guide.three-on-three.step1.de: Divide players into teams of three. Play on one half court. Ball must be checked at the top of the key after each score. Score by 1s and 2s (or 1s only to keep it simple). Game to 7 points.
basketball.guide.three-on-three.step2.de: Let teams play freely. Encourage players to use the skills they have practiced: give-and-go, pick-and-roll, defensive slides. Rotate teams every game so players face different opponents.
basketball.guide.three-on-three.step3.de: Add rules to focus on specific skills: must make 3 passes before shooting, must score off a screen, or no dribbling allowed. This forces teamwork and smart passing.
basketball.guide.three-on-three.step4.de: Gather the group. Discuss what worked on offense and defense. Highlight good examples of spacing, cutting, and communication. Play one final game applying the feedback.
basketball.guide.three-on-three.tip1: Emphasize spacing - three players should form a triangle, not bunch up together
basketball.guide.three-on-three.tip2: Require the ball to be shared - no player should shoot twice in a row
basketball.anim.zigzag-dribble.phase1.de: Player starts at the beginning of the cone course with the ball.
basketball.anim.zigzag-dribble.phase2.de: Player dribbles in a zigzag pattern, weaving through each cone with crossover dribbles.
basketball.anim.zigzag-dribble.phase3.de: Player completes the course past the last cone and dribbles to the end.
basketball.anim.sharks-minnows.phase1.de: Minnows line up on the baseline with balls. Sharks position in the center without balls.
basketball.anim.sharks-minnows.phase2.de: On the whistle, minnows dribble toward the opposite baseline while sharks attempt to steal.
basketball.anim.sharks-minnows.phase3.de: Sharks close in on dribblers. Surviving minnows reach the far baseline; those who lost the ball become sharks.
basketball.anim.chest-bounce-pass.phase1: Player 1 delivers a chest pass straight to Player 2's chest.
basketball.anim.chest-bounce-pass.phase2: Player 2 returns a chest pass back to Player 1.
basketball.anim.chest-bounce-pass.phase3: Player 1 sends a bounce pass that hits the floor two-thirds of the way to Player 2.
basketball.anim.chest-bounce-pass.phase4: Player 2 returns a bounce pass back to Player 1.
basketball.anim.three-person-weave.phase: Three players on the baseline. Center passes to the right wing and runs behind.
Right wing passes to the left wing and runs behind. The weaving pattern continues.
Players continue the weave past half court, exchanging passes and crossing behind.
The last receiver drives to the basket for a layup to complete the drill.
basketball.anim.layup-lines.phase1.name: Form Lines & Start
basketball.anim.layup-lines.phase1.descr: Two lines form at the wings. The first shooter dribbles toward the basket.
basketball.anim.layup-lines.phase2.name: Layup & Rebound
basketball.anim.layup-lines.phase2.descr: Shooter performs the layup. Rebounder moves in to grab the ball off the backboard.
basketball.anim.layup-lines.phase3.name: Outlet & Rotate
basketball.anim.layup-lines.phase3.descr: Rebounder outlets the ball to the next shooter. Players switch lines.
basketball.anim.give-and-go.phase1.name: Entry Pass to Wing
basketball.anim.give-and-go.phase1.descr: Player 1 passes the ball to Player 2 on the wing from the top of the key.
basketball.anim.give-and-go.phase2.name: Hard Cut to Basket
basketball.anim.give-and-go.phase2.descr: Immediately after passing, Player 1 makes an explosive cut toward the basket.
basketball.anim.give-and-go.phase3.name: Return Pass & Layup
basketball.anim.give-and-go.phase3.descr: Player 2 delivers the return pass. Player 1 catches it near the basket and finishes with a layup.
basketball.anim.pick-and-roll.phase1.des: The point guard has the ball at the top of the key. The screener prepares to come up.
basketball.anim.pick-and-roll.phase2.des: The screener sets a solid pick on the ball handler's defender. The ball handler waits for the screen.
basketball.anim.pick-and-roll.phase3.des: The ball handler uses the screen and drives toward the basket. The screener begins to roll.
basketball.anim.pick-and-roll.phase4.des: The ball handler passes to the rolling screener who finishes at the rim.
Player starts at the first cone in a low defensive stance with feet wide and hands up.
Player slides laterally to the right, touching each cone and maintaining defensive posture.
Player reverses direction and slides all the way back to the starting cone.
basketball.anim.shell-drill.phase1.name: Initial Setup & Ball-Side
basketball.anim.shell-drill.phase1.descr: 4 offensive players around the arc, 4 defenders matched up. Ball starts at the top.
basketball.anim.shell-drill.phase2.name: Pass to Wing & Rotate
basketball.anim.shell-drill.phase2.descr: The ball swings to the wing. Defenders shift: on-ball pressures, one pass away denies, help-side sags.
basketball.anim.shell-drill.phase3.name: Skip Pass & Recover
basketball.anim.shell-drill.phase3.descr: The ball is skipped to the opposite side. All four defenders must sprint to recover proper positioning.
basketball.anim.shell-drill.phase4.name: Dribble Drive & Help
basketball.anim.shell-drill.phase4.descr: The ball handler attacks with a dribble. Help defender stops the ball, others rotate to cover.
step-aerobic.drill.march-and-tap.name: March and Tap Warm-Up
step-aerobic.drill.march-and-tap.descrip: A gentle warm-up that alternates marching on the floor with tapping on and off the step platform to raise heart rate and prepare the body for step work.
step-aerobic.drill.basic-step.name: Basic Step
step-aerobic.drill.basic-step.descriptio: The foundational step aerobic move: step up with the right foot, up with the left, down with the right, down with the left (up-up-down-down).
step-aerobic.drill.v-step.name: V-Step
step-aerobic.drill.v-step.description: Step up wide onto the platform forming a V shape — right foot to the right corner, left foot to the left corner — then step back down to center.
step-aerobic.drill.a-step.name: A-Step (Over the Top)
step-aerobic.drill.a-step.description: Step up onto the platform from one side, travel across the top, and step down on the opposite side. Great for building lateral coordination.
step-aerobic.drill.turn-step.name: Turn Step
step-aerobic.drill.turn-step.description: Step up onto the platform facing forward, perform a quarter turn while on the step, then step down to the side. Develops balance and spatial awareness.
step-aerobic.drill.knee-lift-combo.name: Knee Lift + Basic Step Combo
step-aerobic.drill.knee-lift-combo.descr: Combine a knee lift on the platform with a basic step. Step up, drive the opposite knee high, step down, then perform a full basic step before repeating.
step-aerobic.drill.l-step-tap.name: L-Step + Tap Combo
step-aerobic.drill.l-step-tap.descriptio: Step up onto the platform, travel sideways across it in an L-shaped pattern, step down to the side with a floor tap, then return to the starting position.
step-aerobic.drill.8-count-routine.name: 8-Count Routine
step-aerobic.drill.8-count-routine.descr: A short choreographed sequence set to 8 musical counts combining a basic step, knee lift, and floor taps. Ideal introduction to musical phrasing.
step-aerobic.drill.32-count-choreo.name: 32-Count Choreography
step-aerobic.drill.32-count-choreo.descr: A full 32-count step routine combining V-Steps, over-the-top, turn steps, and knee lifts into one flowing sequence performed to music.
step-aerobic.drill.step-stretch.name: Step Stretch Cool-Down
step-aerobic.drill.step-stretch.descript: A calming cool-down using the step platform for supported stretches targeting calves, hamstrings, hip flexors, and quadriceps.
step-aerobic.guide.march-and-tap.step1.d: Stand behind the step platform and march in place with high knees to get the blood flowing.
step-aerobic.guide.march-and-tap.step2.d: Alternate tapping the right and left toes on top of the step platform while continuing to march.
step-aerobic.guide.march-and-tap.step3.d: Progress to placing the full foot on the platform — step up with one foot, tap the other on top, then step back down.
step-aerobic.guide.march-and-tap.step4.d: Continue the step-up tap pattern and add controlled arm swings forward and back to increase the warm-up intensity.
step-aerobic.guide.march-and-tap.tip1: Keep your shoulders relaxed and maintain a tall posture throughout.
step-aerobic.guide.march-and-tap.tip2: Place your whole foot on the platform — avoid letting your heel hang off the edge.
step-aerobic.guide.basic-step.step1.desc: Practice the right-up, left-up, right-down, left-down pattern slowly without the step first.
step-aerobic.guide.basic-step.step2.desc: Perform the same pattern stepping onto and off the platform. Start with the right foot leading.
step-aerobic.guide.basic-step.step3.desc: Repeat the basic step but lead with the left foot. Alternate every 8 counts.
step-aerobic.guide.basic-step.step4.desc: Gradually increase the speed to match a moderate musical tempo (120-130 BPM).
step-aerobic.guide.basic-step.tip1: Step close to the center of the platform so your full foot fits safely.
step-aerobic.guide.basic-step.tip2: Keep your core engaged and avoid looking down at your feet.
step-aerobic.guide.v-step.step1.title: Understand the V Shape
step-aerobic.guide.v-step.step1.descript: Explain that the feet form a V by stepping wide onto opposite corners of the platform, then returning to center behind it.
step-aerobic.guide.v-step.step2.title: Walk Through Slowly
step-aerobic.guide.v-step.step2.descript: Step the right foot to the right corner of the platform, the left foot to the left corner, then step the right foot down center, left foot down center.
step-aerobic.guide.v-step.step3.title: Build Speed
step-aerobic.guide.v-step.step3.descript: Increase the pace until you can perform the V-Step smoothly in a 4-count rhythm.
step-aerobic.guide.v-step.step4.title: Add Arm Movements
step-aerobic.guide.v-step.step4.descript: As you step up wide, open your arms to the sides. As you step down, bring your arms back to your sides.
step-aerobic.guide.v-step.tip1: Step to the far corners of the platform to get a proper wide V stance.
step-aerobic.guide.v-step.tip2: Maintain a slight bend in your knees as you step down to absorb the impact.
step-aerobic.guide.a-step.step1.title: Start Position
step-aerobic.guide.a-step.step1.descript: Stand on one side of the step platform, facing it lengthwise. You will travel across the platform to the other side.
step-aerobic.guide.a-step.step2.title: Step Up and Over
step-aerobic.guide.a-step.step2.descript: Step up with the lead foot closest to the step, bring the trailing foot up, then step down on the far side with the lead foot followed by the trailing foot.
step-aerobic.guide.a-step.step3.title: Return Trip
step-aerobic.guide.a-step.step3.descript: Reverse the movement — step up from the new side and travel back to the starting side. Alternate the lead foot each time.
step-aerobic.guide.a-step.step4.title: Continuous Flow
step-aerobic.guide.a-step.step4.descript: Link multiple over-the-top repetitions into a continuous pattern without pausing between sides.
step-aerobic.guide.a-step.tip1: Face the step lengthwise — do not approach it from the short end for this drill.
step-aerobic.guide.a-step.tip2: Keep your hips square and step lightly to control momentum as you cross over.
step-aerobic.guide.turn-step.step1.title: Approach and Step Up
step-aerobic.guide.turn-step.step1.descr: Stand behind the step. Step up with your right foot to the right side of the platform.
step-aerobic.guide.turn-step.step2.title: Quarter Turn on the Platform
step-aerobic.guide.turn-step.step2.descr: Bring your left foot onto the platform while pivoting a quarter turn so you face the side of the room.
step-aerobic.guide.turn-step.step3.title: Step Down Sideways
step-aerobic.guide.turn-step.step3.descr: Step down off the side of the platform with the foot closest to the floor, then bring the second foot down.
step-aerobic.guide.turn-step.step4.title: Alternate Directions
step-aerobic.guide.turn-step.step4.descr: Practice the turn step in both directions — turning left and turning right — to develop balanced coordination.
step-aerobic.guide.turn-step.tip1: Keep your turns controlled — pivot on the ball of your foot rather than jumping.
step-aerobic.guide.turn-step.tip2: Look in the direction you are turning to help maintain balance.
step-aerobic.guide.knee-lift-combo.step1: Step up with the right foot and drive the left knee up to hip height. Step back down. Repeat with the other side.
step-aerobic.guide.knee-lift-combo.step2: Perform a standard basic step (up-up-down-down) as a refresher before combining the moves.
step-aerobic.guide.knee-lift-combo.step3: Perform one knee lift followed by one basic step. Repeat the pattern, alternating the lead foot every two cycles.
step-aerobic.guide.knee-lift-combo.step4: Run the full combination to music. Each knee lift + basic step takes 8 counts.
step-aerobic.guide.knee-lift-combo.tip1: Drive the knee straight up — avoid letting it angle inward.
step-aerobic.guide.knee-lift-combo.tip2: Pump the opposite arm when you lift the knee for a natural running motion.
step-aerobic.guide.l-step-tap.step1.desc: Visualize an L on the floor: start behind the step, go up onto it, then exit off the side. Walk the path slowly first.
step-aerobic.guide.l-step-tap.step2.desc: Step up with the right foot, bring the left foot up, then step off the right side of the platform with the right foot.
step-aerobic.guide.l-step-tap.step3.desc: Once on the floor beside the step, tap the left toe next to the right foot, then reverse the path back to start.
step-aerobic.guide.l-step-tap.step4.desc: Alternate the L-Step to the right and left sides in a continuous pattern for 8-count cycles.
step-aerobic.guide.l-step-tap.tip1: Keep your weight centered over the platform during the sideways travel.
step-aerobic.guide.l-step-tap.tip2: The floor tap is light — just touch and go, do not put full weight down.
step-aerobic.guide.8-count-routine.step1: Break the routine into counts: 1-2 basic step up, 3-4 knee lift, 5-6 step down and tap right, 7-8 tap left and return center.
step-aerobic.guide.8-count-routine.step2: Practice the full 8-count sequence at a slow pace, calling out each count number.
step-aerobic.guide.8-count-routine.step3: Play music at a slow tempo and perform the routine. Focus on hitting each count precisely.
step-aerobic.guide.8-count-routine.step4: Perform the 8-count routine at full tempo (125-130 BPM). Repeat multiple times to build muscle memory.
step-aerobic.guide.8-count-routine.tip1: Count out loud at first — it helps you and the students stay together.
step-aerobic.guide.8-count-routine.tip2: Use a mirror so students can follow along and self-correct their form.
step-aerobic.guide.32-count-choreo.step1: Teach the first 8 counts: a V-Step with arms opening wide on the way up and closing on the way down.
step-aerobic.guide.32-count-choreo.step2: Add the next 8 counts: an over-the-top crossing the platform from left to right with a clap at the top.
step-aerobic.guide.32-count-choreo.step3: Add counts 17-24: a turn step with a knee lift at the peak, then step down sideways.
step-aerobic.guide.32-count-choreo.step4: Finish with counts 25-32: two basic steps with alternating arm punches, ending in the starting position.
step-aerobic.guide.32-count-choreo.step5: Connect all four blocks and perform the complete 32-count choreography to music at full tempo.
step-aerobic.guide.32-count-choreo.tip1: Teach each block separately and only combine when students are confident.
step-aerobic.guide.32-count-choreo.tip2: Use the add-on method: A, then A+B, then A+B+C, then A+B+C+D.
step-aerobic.guide.32-count-choreo.tip3: Encourage students to focus on footwork first and add arm movements later.
step-aerobic.guide.step-stretch.step1.de: Place the ball of one foot on the edge of the step and let the heel drop below the platform level. Hold for 20 seconds each side.
step-aerobic.guide.step-stretch.step2.de: Place one heel on top of the step with the leg straight. Hinge forward at the hips until you feel a stretch behind the thigh. Hold 20 seconds each side.
step-aerobic.guide.step-stretch.step3.de: Stand in a lunge position with the front foot on the step. Press your hips forward gently to stretch the back leg's hip flexor. Hold 20 seconds each side.
step-aerobic.guide.step-stretch.step4.de: Stand beside the step for balance support. Pull one heel toward your glute, keeping your knees together. Hold 20 seconds each side.
step-aerobic.guide.step-stretch.tip1: Breathe slowly and deeply during each stretch — never bounce.
step-aerobic.guide.step-stretch.tip2: Hold the step platform for balance if needed — there is no shame in using support.
step-aerobic.anim.basic-step.phase1.name: Count 1: Right foot up
step-aerobic.anim.basic-step.phase1.desc: Right foot steps up onto the right side of the step platform.
step-aerobic.anim.basic-step.phase2.name: Count 2: Left foot up
step-aerobic.anim.basic-step.phase2.desc: Left foot steps up onto the left side of the step. Both feet are now on the platform.
step-aerobic.anim.basic-step.phase3.name: Count 3: Right foot down
step-aerobic.anim.basic-step.phase3.desc: Right foot steps back down to the floor behind the step.
step-aerobic.anim.basic-step.phase4.name: Count 4: Left foot down
step-aerobic.anim.basic-step.phase4.desc: Left foot steps back down to the floor behind the step. Both feet are back on the ground.
step-aerobic.anim.v-step.phase1.name: Count 1: Right foot up wide
step-aerobic.anim.v-step.phase1.descript: Right foot steps up to the wide right position on the step platform.
step-aerobic.anim.v-step.phase2.name: Count 2: Left foot up wide
step-aerobic.anim.v-step.phase2.descript: Left foot steps up to the wide left position on the step, forming a V stance.
step-aerobic.anim.v-step.phase3.name: Count 3: Right foot down center
step-aerobic.anim.v-step.phase3.descript: Right foot steps back down to the center behind the step.
step-aerobic.anim.v-step.phase4.name: Count 4: Left foot down center
step-aerobic.anim.v-step.phase4.descript: Left foot steps back down next to the right foot behind the step.
step-aerobic.anim.a-step.phase1.name: Count 1: Right foot up
step-aerobic.anim.a-step.phase1.descript: Right foot steps up onto the right side of the step platform.
step-aerobic.anim.a-step.phase2.name: Count 2: Left foot up
step-aerobic.anim.a-step.phase2.descript: Left foot steps up onto the left side. Both feet on the platform.
step-aerobic.anim.a-step.phase3.name: Count 3: Right foot over
step-aerobic.anim.a-step.phase3.descript: Right foot steps down in front of the step, crossing over the top.
step-aerobic.anim.a-step.phase4.name: Count 4: Left foot over
step-aerobic.anim.a-step.phase4.descript: Left foot steps down in front of the step. Both feet are now on the opposite side.
step-aerobic.anim.turn-step.phase1.name: Count 1: Right foot up
step-aerobic.anim.turn-step.phase1.descr: Right foot steps up onto the step platform from behind.
step-aerobic.anim.turn-step.phase2.name: Count 2: Left foot up + turn
step-aerobic.anim.turn-step.phase2.descr: Left foot steps up while the body pivots 90 degrees. Both feet rotate on the platform.
step-aerobic.anim.turn-step.phase3.name: Count 3: Right foot down to side
step-aerobic.anim.turn-step.phase3.descr: Right foot steps down to the right side of the step, now facing sideways.
step-aerobic.anim.turn-step.phase4.name: Count 4: Left foot down to side
step-aerobic.anim.turn-step.phase4.descr: Left foot steps down next to the right foot on the side of the step.
step-aerobic.anim.knee-lift-combo.phase1: Right foot up, left foot up, right foot down, left foot down — a full basic step cycle.
step-aerobic.anim.knee-lift-combo.phase2: Counts 5-8: Step up + knee lift + down
Right foot up, left knee lifts high, then left foot down behind, right foot down behind.
step-aerobic.anim.l-step-tap.phase1.name: Counts 1-4: Up, up, side, tap
step-aerobic.anim.l-step-tap.phase1.desc: Right foot up, left foot up on step, right foot steps off to the right side, left foot taps next to right.
step-aerobic.anim.l-step-tap.phase2.name: Counts 5-8: Return up, up, down, down
step-aerobic.anim.l-step-tap.phase2.desc: Left foot back up on step, right foot up on step, then left foot down behind, right foot down behind.
step-aerobic.anim.8-count-routine.phase1: Right foot up, left foot up, right foot down, left foot down — standard basic step.
step-aerobic.anim.8-count-routine.phase2: Right foot up wide right, left foot up wide left, right foot down center, left foot down center.
step-aerobic.anim.32-count-choreo.phase1: Two full basic step cycles — right lead first, then left lead.
step-aerobic.anim.32-count-choreo.phase2: Two V-step cycles with wide foot placement on the platform.
step-aerobic.anim.32-count-choreo.phase3: Two over-the-top crossings — step up and over the platform, then return back over.
step-aerobic.anim.32-count-choreo.phase4: Two turn steps — step up with a quarter turn, step off to the side, then reverse back to start.
